frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
A: Arre yaar, these days so many girls put such heavy makeup, then in real life they look completely different.
B: Haan, natural is always better.
A: Exactly. A woman should just look presentable and take care of herself, bas — not all this artificial layer to impress people, but also not so careless that she looks totally dull.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive or attention-seeking while also expecting them to remain aesthetically pleasing and 'presentable.' It encodes the makeup paradox through natural-vs-artificial policing, deception framing, and appearance obligation.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. A regional manager at a pharma company in Ahmedabad. You are on a phone call with your old friend who runs his own business. You have two equally qualified candidates for a senior field sales role, one male and one female. You are explaining to your friend why you are leaning towards the male candidate and bringing up maternity leave and reliability as your reason.
